title: hopea altocollina
intro: Hopea altocollina, also known as the Hill White Meranti, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and triterpenes, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hopea altocollina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Hopea altocollina often involve the bark being dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications.
